title: Reasons for determination — July 3, 2026
summary: Canadian manufacturers of certain steel products face new dumping duties after the CITT found foreign competitors were selling below cost, making their own exports less competitive.

title: Determination — June 18, 2026
summary: Canadian companies exporting steel products to the U.S. now face new tariffs after the CITT confirmed increased duties are justified to protect domestic steel makers from import competition.

title: Determination — June 9, 2026
summary: Canadian manufacturers importing steel pipes must now pay new anti-dumping duties ranging from 15-35% because the Canadian International Trade Tribunal found unfair pricing from foreign competitors.

title: Public hearing — May 25, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal is holding a public hearing on May 25, 2026, where Canadian businesses can present evidence about trade disputes or tariff matters directly affecting their operations.

title: Determination — May 15, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal ruled that imported steel products no longer qualify for tariff relief, meaning Canadian businesses will now pay higher import duties on these goods.
